commit.message:
Security hardening: capture engine fixes and viewer response sanitization (#3880)

Capture:
- Fix crash when corruptSavePcap re-dispatches packets that already
  exceeded tunnelDepth limit — reset tunnelDepth before CORRUPT
  ethernet callback so mProtocol gets set (fixes NULL deref)
- Fix js0n bounds check: callers pass sizeof(out) in bytes but js0n
  used it as element count, making limit 4x too generous (ALW)
- Fix wise.c domain string corruption: restore colon before two
  early-return paths in wise_lookup_domain
- Right-size TLS JA3 stack buffers based on protocol constraints
  (ja3 30000→13000, eja3 10000→13000, ecja3 10000→13000, ecfja3 1000→1024)
- Prevent symlink attacks in writer-simple: unlink before open with
  O_EXCL to avoid TOCTOU races on pcap output files
- Enlarge AWS credential buffers from 100 to 256 in S3/SQS readers
- Use dynamic allocation for S3 escape and continuation token URIs
  to prevent truncation with long tokens
- Improve signal handler safety: set exit handler first in controlc
  and terminate so double-signal always terminates

Viewer:
- Extend prototype pollution middleware to check req.body
- Set Content-Type text/plain on error responses across CSV, session,
  connection, cyberchef, bodyhash, spigraph, and tag endpoints to
  prevent browsers from interpreting error text as HTML
- Prevent mass-assignment in user layout update endpoints by
  whitelisting allowed fields from req.body
- Stricter regex validation for history index parameter
- Fix hunt role access check: !arr.length was truthy for empty arrays

Tests:
- Add coverage for text/plain error responses and updated endpoints

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: ffflorian <5497598+ffflorian@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(ci): rendre les tests Vitest bloquants (#84)

## Objet

Les tests Vitest étaient non-bloquants dans le CI — une PR pouvait être
mergée même avec des tests unitaires en échec. Suppression du
`continue-on-error` et du fallback `|| echo`.

**Avant :**
```yaml
- name: Test
  run: npm run test:ci --if-present || echo "Tests skipped or failed (non-blocking)"
  continue-on-error: true
```

**Après :**
```yaml
- name: Test
  run: npm run test:ci
```

## Checklist
- [x] Convention de commit respectée
- [x] Lint OK / Type-check OK / Tests OK
- [ ] Docs/README mis à jour si nécessaire
- [x] Breaking changes documentées ou aucune
- [x] Tests ajoutés ou N/A
- [x] Portée petite et réversible

## Notes
Changement limité à un seul step dans `ci.yml`. Aucun autre fichier
modifié.

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: stephanedenis <10110359+stephanedenis@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix parsing of if expressions within binary operators (#84)

* Initial plan

* Fix if-expression parsing to work within binary operators

Move if-expression handling from parse_expression to parse_primary so
that if expressions participate in the normal operator precedence chain.
This allows expressions like:
  y += if foo { 1 } else { 0 } + if bar { -1 } else { 0 };

Add test script and vscode grammar test for if-in-binop expressions.

commit.message:
Add cross-runtime SQLite adapters and runtime smoke coverage (#4)

* Add cross-runtime SQLite adapters and runtime smoke coverage

* Improve JS vector fallback with top-K heap and fix smoke-bun CI Node setup

- Replace O(N log N) full-sort in searchByVectorInJavaScript with a
  bounded max-heap (O(N log K) time, O(K) memory) to avoid loading and
  sorting all embeddings when sqlite-vector is unavailable
- Add limit <= 0 early-return guard and remove unreachable nullish
  coalescing on heap[0] now that the guard guarantees heap is non-empty
- Add actions/setup-node (Node 24, pnpm cache) to the smoke-bun CI job
  to match the pinned environment used in verify and live-tests jobs

commit.message:
🗃️ fix: healthz no-reload refresh + intent filter toy exception 🗃️

healthz: replace location.reload() with fetch+DOM update
- Full page reload every 30s was causing HTTP/2 GOAWAY frames that
aborted SSE connections on other tabs (search results stream)
- On Tor Browser the reload also triggered Onion-Location redirect
to .onion, failing for users without a Tor proxy configured
- New approach: fetch('/healthz.json', {cache:'no-store'}) on each
30s cycle; applyUpdate() patches status banner, checks table,
stats, and timestamp in-place — no navigation, no connection abort
- Falls back to location.reload() only if the JSON endpoint is
unreachable (server down scenario)
- Added id="hz-timestamp" on <time> for targeted DOM update
taxonomy.go: toy-word exception for ambiguous male indicators
- Added toyWords list (dildo/strapon/strap-on/vibrator/toy/artificial)
- When title contains a toy word, cock/cocks/dick/dicks/bbc are
treated as toy references and skipped from male-presence check
- Handles real cases: "strapon dick", "bbc dildo", "artificial cock"
- Verified against 19 live title cases: 0 false positives
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: domain-boundary guard and per-request proxy addr for CORS

Two bugs fixed found during QA loop across 10 real websites:

1. Domain-boundary corruption (c.s-microsoft.com bug):
   - Old: strings.ReplaceAll(s, targetHost, proxyAddr) was a blind
     substring match, corrupting 'c.s-microsoft.com' when target=microsoft.com
   - Fix: bareTargetRe uses (?i)(^|[^-a-zA-Z0-9.])HOST([^-a-zA-Z0-9.]|$)
     so only exact domain boundary matches are rewritten.

2. CORS errors from 127.0.0.1 vs localhost redirect mismatch:
   - When browser connects via 127.0.0.1:PORT but redirect Location is
     rewritten to localhost:PORT, following the redirect is cross-origin
     -> CORS blocked.
   - Fix: thread original client Host through request context; use it as
     effectiveProxyAddr in modifyResponse (with port-matching guard to
     prevent false matches in tests).

Tests: 38 pass, added TestMaskResponseStringDomainBoundary regression test.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: snapshot rendering crash with clustered lighting (#18293)

## Bug

Enabling snapshot rendering (both STANDARD and FAST modes) while
clustered lighting is active causes continuous WebGPU validation errors:

```
Recording in [CommandEncoder "RenderEncoder"] which is locked while
[RenderPassEncoder "TileMaskTexture - RenderPass"] is open.
 - While encoding ClearBuffer(...)
```

**Forum report:** https://forum.babylonjs.com/t/63139
**Repro:** https://playground.babylonjs.com/#QAD7FB#1 (STANDARD),
https://playground.babylonjs.com/#QAD7FB#2 (FAST)

## Root Cause

`clearStorageBuffer()` calls `_renderEncoder.clearBuffer()`, which is a
command encoder operation that **cannot** execute while a render pass is
open on the same encoder.

In snapshot rendering mode, `bindFramebuffer()` eagerly creates the
render pass (unlike normal mode which defers it). The clustered lighting
system then fires `onClearObservable` — which calls
`StorageBuffer.clear()` — while the render pass is still open, violating
the WebGPU spec.

## Fix

1. **Clustered lighting** (`clusteredLightContainer.ts`): Move the
WebGPU storage buffer clear from `onClearObservable` (fires after
`bindFramebuffer` creates the render pass) to `onBeforeBindObservable`
(fires before), so it runs while the command encoder is free. This also
preserves bundle count synchronization for FAST mode snapshot rendering.

2. **Engine safety guard** (`webgpuEngine.ts`): Add
`_endCurrentRenderPass()` in `clearStorageBuffer()` to prevent the same
class of bug for any future callers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: add save confirmation and move profiles to top of settings

Two UX issues in EndpointSettingsView:

1. No save feedback — saving/updating/deleting a profile gave no
   visual confirmation. Added a green success banner at the top of
   the form that auto-dismisses after 3 seconds.

2. Profiles hidden at bottom — the Saved Profiles list was the last
   section, below 6+ form sections, invisible without scrolling.
   Moved it to the top so existing connections are immediately
   visible when entering the settings screen.

Also moved the error banner to the top alongside the success banner
for consistent feedback placement.

at 5 results. Search input uses p5.dom createInput() positioned
relative to canvas. draw() is driven by noLoop/redraw so filtering
only happens in the input handler, not every frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

tui: truncate permission description and simplify tool input display

Permission description fix:
- executor.rs was serializing the ENTIRE tool input JSON into the
  permission description (e.g. full file contents for Write tool).
  This made the permission prompt description hundreds of lines long,
  breaking TUI layout.
- New build_perm_description() extracts only short, human-readable
  fields (command, file_path, path, pattern, url, etc.) and truncates
  values to 80 chars.

ToolUseReady simplification:
- Previously showed up to 3 key=value pairs with 40-char truncation,
  which was still noisy and could produce long lines.
- Now shows only the first relevant field (command, file_path, etc.)
  with 60-char truncation. User only needs to know which tool is
  running and on what target.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Initial commit

commit.message:
fix: stream file primitive hashing

Replace full-file reads in models hashing paths with buffered streaming SHA-256 so file primitive collection and file-backed content digests keep memory bounded while preserving identical hashes.

Add tests for large multi-chunk files and for file-backed content_digest fallback behavior.

feat: add MCP per-tool authorization filters (Phase 3) (#81)

Implements ToolAuthorizationFilter (tools/call) and ToolListAuthorizationFilter
(tools/list) that enforce per-tool authorization policies. When Authentication.Enabled
is false the filters are never registered — zero behaviour change for existing
deployments.

- AuthorizationHelpers: HasRequiredScopes, HasRequiredRoles, GetToolOverride
- ToolAuthorizationFilter: blocks calls to tools based on FunctionOverride / default
  policy; returns MCP error result with 'Authentication required' or 'Insufficient
  permissions' messages
- ToolListAuthorizationFilter: strips tools the caller cannot invoke from tools/list
  responses
- Program.cs: wires both filters via WithRequestFilters when auth is enabled

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix timeline empty: add timeout + background refresh for dashboard API calls

Root cause: Dashboard API calls go through the shared rate limiter semaphore.
When agents saturate the semaphore, the dashboard's 15s timeout fires and
returns empty data (no cache on fresh start).

Fix:
- DashboardDataService: 8s timeout on API calls with OperationCanceledException
  handling that returns cached data + schedules a background refresh
- Background refresh fires after 15s delay (when semaphore contention clears)
- Timeline page: auto-retries every 15s when empty (not just rate-limited)
- Increased timeline page timeout to 20s to accommodate the new flow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: permission screen now transitions to StreamView after granting

The PermissionRequestView had no way to notify the parent app that
permissions were granted. checkRequiredPermissions() only ran on
launch and when returning from background, so granting permissions
in-app left the user stuck on the permission screen.

Changes:
- Add onAllRequired callback to PermissionRequestView
- Auto-navigate to StreamView 0.6s after camera + mic are granted
- Show a visible 'Continue to StreamCaster' button as fallback
- StreamCasterApp passes checkRequiredPermissions as the callback

Implement remaining 20 features: wallpaper, list mode, loadable filter, image save, dimension filter, filelist I/O, HiDPI, multi-monitor, file watching, custom actions, captions, keybindings, thumbnail mode, HTTP fetch, lossless rotate, multi-window, index mode, interactive modes

- Caption file support (--caption-path)
- Configurable keybindings (--key-binding)
- Thumbnail grid mode (-t) with keyboard/mouse navigation

- Lossless rotation via WIC transform + save

commit.message:
Potential fix for pull request finding

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add 3 orthogonal spot trading strategies: vol mean-reversion, cross-sectional arb, microstructure flow (#7)

* Add Volatility Mean-Reversion strategy with Garman-Klass estimator

Implements a novel spot-only strategy that exploits the mean-reverting
nature of volatility in crypto markets using:

- Garman-Klass (1980) OHLC volatility estimator for efficient vol
  estimation from full bar range data
- Vol z-score gating (short vs long GK vol displacement)
- Bollinger Band / Keltner Channel squeeze detection and release
- Composite mean-reversion signal (RSI + VWAP deviation + vol-regime)
- Bayesian HMM regime gating via MarkovRegimeDetector
- Volatility-target position sizing with ATR stops, drawdown circuit
  breaker, and single-instrument crash guard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address review: fix vol-scalar fallback and use Decimal.quantize

- Skip instruments with zero realised vol instead of using vol_target
  as a misleading fallback
- Use Decimal.quantize() instead of Decimal(str(round(float(...)))) for
  cleaner precision control

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Cross-Sectional Statistical Arbitrage strategy

Implement a pure cross-sectional stat-arb strategy that ranks all
instruments against each other using six orthogonal factors (relative
momentum, volume surprise, mean-reversion residual, liquidity premium,
funding carry, volatility discount). Key features:

- Rolling OLS beta decomposition against volume-weighted market return
- Rank-based composite scoring with cross-sectional normalisation
- Beta-hedged long-only portfolio construction
- Markov regime gating (full breadth → flight-to-quality → flat)
- Cross-sectional dispersion filter
- Herfindahl concentration check, ATR stop-losses, drawdown circuit
  breaker, and minimum-rank-change turnover control

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Microstructure / Order Flow strategy with funding rate spillover

Implements Strategy C: a microstructure-focused alpha that derives
high-frequency order-flow proxies from OHLCV data and combines them
with perpetual funding-rate spillover signals.

Key components:
- Volume Imbalance Ratio (VIR) acceleration for buy/sell pressure
- VPIN proxy for flow toxicity gating
- Funding rate contrarian + momentum signals
- Price impact asymmetry detection
- Amihud illiquidity acceleration
- Markov regime gating with four-state exposure scaling
- ATR stop-losses, drawdown circuit breaker, crash guard
- Signal persistence filter (min_signal_bars)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add tests for vol_mean_reversion, cross_sectional_arb, microstructure_flow strategies

72 tests across 3 files covering:
- Helper function unit tests (GK variance, ATR, vol, buy/sell volume, VPIN)
- Portfolio construction (weight caps, gross exposure, non-negative weights)
- Edge cases (empty input, insufficient data, zero price/volume)
- Regime gating and crisis exposure
- Stop-loss and drawdown circuit breaker
- Factory functions
- BacktestEngine integration

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: YuTsyh <51702981+YuTsyh@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

feat: expand reader group to all project resources

Reader group now gets read-only access across every resource
provisioned by Terraform, not just the AKS cluster:

- Subscription: Reader (ARM control-plane visibility on all resources)
- AKS cluster: Azure Kubernetes Service RBAC Reader (k8s data-plane)
- ACR: AcrPull (pull images, no push)
- Key Vault: Key Vault Reader (metadata only, no secret values)

Admin group assignments are unchanged.

commit.message:
benchmark: add v0.10.8 three-arm baseline results (Arm A + Arm B)

Arm A (standard prompt): avg 45.50 (Opus 46, Sonnet 44, GPT-5.4 46, Codex 46)
Arm B (terse overlay): avg 45.00 (Opus 46, Sonnet 44, GPT-5.4 45, Codex 45)

Key findings:
- Terse overlay has slight negative effect (-0.50 avg), rejecting brevity-only hypothesis
- MFA Clarity is the most sensitive dimension to terse overlay (GPT models drop 9→8)
- Opus is immune to terse overlay; Sonnet is consistently lowest scorer
- Continuation logic unanimously identified as highest-risk MFA component

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Update infra/main.bicep

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: adaptive control bar layout for portrait and landscape

The control bar had 7 buttons in a fixed-spacing HStack that needed
~524pt but portrait iPhones only provide 320-430pt, causing edge
buttons to be clipped off-screen.

Changes:
- Portrait: two-row layout with secondary buttons on top row and
  the start/stop button centered on the bottom row
- Landscape: single row with flexible Spacer(minLength:0) between
  each button so they distribute evenly across available width
- Normalize minimalModeButton to 44x44 frame (was using padding(12))
- Fix double-padding: VStack uses .padding(.vertical) only, control
  bar and HUD manage their own horizontal padding independently

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
